verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70-1A) is canonically a cytosolic/nuclear stress-inducible chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or signal peptide. However, there is well-documented gene-specific evidence of cell-surface display of HSP70 on stressed and tumor cells (membrane Hsp70, mHsp70), detected by flow cytometry on intact cells (cmHsp70.1 antibody), surface biotinylation, and imaging. Surface Hsp70 on tumor cells is the target of clinical-stage agents (e.g., cmHsp70.1-based approaches, TKD peptide NK-cell therapies). Outer-leaflet display is associated with binding to phosphatidylserine/Bag-4 and lipid rafts under stress/transformation. This is classic cell-state-induced ecto-presentation, not baseline surface residency.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Exact molecular mechanism of outer-leaflet anchoring (lipid binding vs partner-mediated) remains debated but surface display itself is well documented.
